2026-04-22T01:41:49.9852125Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9852671Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9853112Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9853478Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9854248Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9854601Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9854793Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9854972Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9855141Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9855371Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9855572Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9855855Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T01:41:49.9856112Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The commit added four resource types — `ComputeLimitFeatureResource`, `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`, `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`, and `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` — plus their respective collection and data classes, and a `Disable` operation on `ComputeLimitFeatureResource`.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **Key concepts section** is a generic link with no package-specific explanation; it does not describe the resource hierarchy (`Feature → GuestSubscription → SharedLimit → VmFamily`) or the purpose of the service (sharing compute quota limits across subscriptions)\n- **Examples section** only links to a generic samples page; there are no package-specific code snippets showing how to authenticate, list features, enable/disable a feature, manage guest subscriptions, or manage VM family shared limits\n- **No sample test files** exist under `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/tests/Samples/` — the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling has no source to pull from\n- **README does not mention** the four main resource types added in this GA release (`ComputeLimitFeatureResource`, `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`, `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`, `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource`) or the `Disable`/`Enable` operations\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/README.md` — Edit\n\nReplace the **Key concepts** section content with package-specific information:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit` library manages the following resource types:\n\n| Resource | ARM type | Description |\n|---|---|---|\n| `ComputeLimitFeatureResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features` | A compute limit feature (e.g., shared quota) that can be enabled or disabled per subscription-location |\n| `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features/guestSubscriptions` | A guest subscription that has been granted access to a shared compute limit |\n| `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features/sharedLimits` | A shared quota limit configuration attached to a feature |\n| `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/vmFamilies` | A VM family whose quota can be shared across guest subscriptions |\n\nThe top-level extension entry point is `SubscriptionResource.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location)` and `SubscriptionResource.GetComputeLimitVmFamilies(location)`.\n```\n\nReplace the **Examples** section with snippet placeholders:\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\n### List features in a subscription-location\n\n```C# Snippet:Azure_ResourceManager_ComputeLimit_ListFeatures\n```\n(closing fence here)\n\n### Enable a compute limit feature\n\n```C# Snippet:Azure_ResourceManager_ComputeLimit_EnableFeature\n```\n(closing fence here)\n\n### Disable a compute limit feature\n\n```C# Snippet:Azure_ResourceManager_ComputeLimit_DisableFeature\n```\n(closing fence here)\n\n### Add a guest subscription to a feature\n\n```C# Snippet:Azure_ResourceManager_ComputeLimit_AddGuestSubscription\n```\n(closing fence here)\n\n### List VM families\n\n```C# Snippet:Azure_ResourceManager_ComputeLimit_ListVmFamilies\n```\n(closing fence here)\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nCreate a new test file at `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/tests/Samples/Sample1_GettingStarted.cs`:\n\n```csharp\n// client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\n\n            AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.EastUS;\n            ComputeLimitFeatureCollection features = subscription.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location);\n\n            await foreach (ComputeLimitFeatureResource feature in features)\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine($\"Feature: {feature.Data.Name}, ProvisioningState: {feature.Data.Properties?.ProvisioningState}\");\n            }\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the = await subscription.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location).GetAsync(featureName);\n\n            ArmOperation\u003cOperationStatusResult\u003e operation = await feature.DisableAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Disable operation status: {operation.Value.Status}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only verifying that the sample builds\")]\n        public async Task AddGuestSubscriptionAsync()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:Azure_ResourceManager_ComputeLimit_AddGuestSubscription\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\n\n            AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.EastUS;\n            string featureName = \"SharedQuota\";\n            ComputeLimitFeatureResource feature = await subscription.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location).GetAsync(featureName);\n\n            ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionCollection guestSubscriptions = feature.GetComputeLimitGuestSubscriptions();\n            string guestSubscriptionId = \"\u003cguest-subscription-id\u003e\";\n            ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionData data = new ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionData();\n 2026-04-22T01:44:14.3832933Z server:unified callBackendTool: serverID=safeoutputs, toolName=create_issue, args=map[body:## Documentation Gap
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3832945Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3833150Z **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit`
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3833388Z **Service directory:** `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/`
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3833551Z **Triggered by:** commit 598a6b223 / PR #57628 by @ArcturusZhang
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3833555Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3833636Z ## What Changed
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3833649Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3835186Z The package was promoted to GA (`1.0.0`) in PR #57628. The commit added four resource types — `ComputeLimitFeatureResource`, `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`, `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`, and `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` — plus their respective collection and data classes, and a `Disable` operation on `ComputeLimitFeatureResource`.
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3835201Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3835282Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3835285Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3836310Z - **Key concepts section** is a generic link with no package-specific explanation; it does not describe the resource hierarchy (`Feature → GuestSubscription → SharedLimit → VmFamily`) or the purpose of the service (sharing compute quota limits across subscriptions)
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3837057Z - **Examples section** only links to a generic samples page; there are no package-specific code snippets showing how to authenticate, list features, enable/disable a feature, manage guest subscriptions, or manage VM family shared limits
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3837690Z - **No sample test files** exist under `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/tests/Samples/` — the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling has no source to pull from
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3838558Z - **README does not mention** the four main resource types added in this GA release (`ComputeLimitFeatureResource`, `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`, `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`, `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource`) or the `Disable`/`Enable` operations
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3838713Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3838792Z <details>
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839000Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839005Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839328Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839332Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839415Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839419Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839696Z #### `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/README.md` — Edit
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839701Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839912Z Replace the **Key concepts** section content with package-specific information:
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839916Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3839989Z ```markdown
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3840061Z ## Key concepts
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3840065Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3840313Z The `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit` library manages the following resource types:
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3840328Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3840423Z | Resource | ARM type | Description |
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3840493Z |---|---|---|
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3841086Z | `ComputeLimitFeatureResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features` | A compute limit feature (e.g., shared quota) that can be enabled or disabled per subscription-location |
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3841700Z | `ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features/guestSubscriptions` | A guest subscription that has been granted access to a shared compute limit |
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3842314Z | `ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/features/sharedLimits` | A shared quota limit configuration attached to a feature |
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3842778Z | `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` | `Microsoft.ComputeLimit/locations/vmFamilies` | A VM family whose quota can be shared across guest subscriptions |
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3842795Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3843330Z The top-level extension entry point is `SubscriptionResource.GetComputeLimitFeatures(location)` and `SubscriptionResource.GetComputeLimitVmFamilies(location)`.
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3843400Z ```
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3843404Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3843559Z Replace the **Examples** section with snippet placeholders:
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3843564Z 
2026-04-22T01:44:14.3843633Z ```markdown

2026-04-22T01:44:14.3845960Z
